filters.notes: 1998-09-02 = days_from_civil(1998,9,2) = 10471. Table sorted ascending by l_shipdate so tail blocks pruned by zone map with early-terminate.

columns.name: l_shipdate
columns.note: loaded ONLY for MIXED blocks; skipped for ALL_PASS blocks

data_structures.block_classification: uint8_t block_type[600] — 0=SKIP, 1=ALL_PASS (max_val<=10471), 2=MIXED (min<=10471<max). Built once from zone map before parallel scan.
data_structures.aggregation: double Acc[6] per accumulator field, thread-local Acc[6] arrays merged after scan. Key = rflag_code*2 + lstatus_code in 0..5.

parallelism.notes: Keep dynamic(4) from iter_0 — proven stable. Static schedule hurt iter_1 due to MIXED-block imbalance. No madvise calls — hot data is already in OS page cache; madvise(HUGEPAGE/WILLNEED) caused the iter_1 regression by triggering kernel THP remapping overhead.

indexes.purpose: Three-way block classification: SKIP (min_val > 10471), ALL_PASS (max_val <= 10471), MIXED (min<=10471<max). Early-terminate once min_val > 10471 (ascending sort order). Eliminates l_shipdate reads for ~597 of 600 blocks.

aggregation_detail.accumulator_type: double (NOT long double — long double forces x87 FPU which serializes FP dependency chains to ~40 cycles, suppressing CPU's ability to keep DRAM requests in flight and capping effective bandwidth at ~31 GB/s instead of 150+ GB/s)
aggregation_detail.struct: struct alignas(64) Acc { int64_t cnt=0; double sum_qty=0, sum_base=0, sum_disc_price=0, sum_charge=0, sum_disc=0; }
aggregation_detail.inner_loop_all_pass: No sd[] load, no branch — direct accumulate: d=dis[i]; p=pr[i]; omd=1.0-d; idx=rf[i]*2+ls[i]; agg[idx].sum_qty+=qty[i]; agg[idx].sum_base+=p; agg[idx].sum_disc_price+=p*omd; agg[idx].sum_charge+=p*omd*(1.0+tax[i]); agg[idx].sum_disc+=d; agg[idx].cnt++;
aggregation_detail.inner_loop_mixed: Same as ALL_PASS but guarded by: if (sd[i] > 10471) continue;
aggregation_detail.merge: Sum thread-local Acc[6] element-wise after parallel region — O(6 * nthreads)

output.sort_strategy: std::sort on up to 6 result rows keyed by (rflag_code, lstatus_code) — dict code order matches string alphabetical order
output.format: %.2f for sum_qty/sum_base; %.4f for sum_disc_price; %.6f for sum_charge; %.2f for avg_qty/avg_price/avg_disc; %ld for cnt. Use double format specifiers (%f not %Lf) throughout.

optimization_notes: Root cause (Category A + B): iter_0's inner loop uses long double for sum_disc_price and sum_charge, forcing x87 FPU execution. X87 serializes computation into ~40-cycle dependency chains (1.0L-ld -> lp*omd -> lp*omd*(1+tax)), blocking the CPU's reorder buffer from seeing future memory loads and suppressing hardware prefetch. This limits effective DRAM bandwidth to ~31 GB/s (observed: 2.28 GB / 73ms) versus the hardware's 150+ GB/s capability. Fix (Category B): replace all long double accumulators with double throughout — eliminates x87, enables SSE2/AVX2 for FP operations, shortens dependency chains to ~4-8 cycles, and allows many more concurrent DRAM prefetch requests. Fix (Category A): extend zone map reading to produce a three-way block classification (ALL_PASS/MIXED/SKIP). For ALL_PASS blocks (597 of 600), skip the l_shipdate column load entirely — saves 597*100000*4 = 238.8 MB of DRAM reads and removes the per-row branch, giving the compiler a straight-line inner loop to optimize. For MIXED blocks (~3 of 600), load l_shipdate and apply the per-row predicate as before. Critically excluded: no madvise(WILLNEED/HUGEPAGE) calls — these caused iter_1's regression from 81ms to 112ms by triggering kernel THP remapping and prefetch overhead on already page-cached hot data. Schedule stays dynamic(4) as in iter_0.

correctness_anchors.date_cutoff_meaning: 1998-09-02 = DATE '1998-12-01' - INTERVAL '90' DAY, encoded as days_from_civil(1998,9,2)
correctness_anchors.output_sort: by (rflag_code ASC, lstatus_code ASC) — dict code order matches string alphabetical order
